title: Why you should stop keto and add carbohydrates

chunks.title: Introduction to Ketogenic Diet Problems
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces the issues associated with the ketogenic diet, primarily focusing on the increase of cortisol, a stress hormone, when carbohydrates are limited. This elevation may have negative effects on overall health and aging.

chunks.title: Cortisol and Its Impact on Health
chunks.summary: The discussion highlights a study demonstrating how a low carbohydrate diet can alter cortisol metabolism. It explains the physiological changes linked to high cortisol levels and suggests that dietary choices can influence metabolic health without necessarily using a ketogenic approach.

chunks.title: Methylglyoxal as a Concern
chunks.summary: The speaker describes methylglyoxal, an advanced glycation end product linked to the ketogenic diet. They detail its increased production in individuals on a ketogenic or Atkins diet and discuss the potential harmful effects on health, including accelerated aging.
chunks.transcript: as I mentioned methylglyoxal is a Advanced glycation end product which looks pretty bad for humans this is an interesting paper ketosis leads to increased methylglyoxal production on the Atkins diet you can see here very clearly stated in this paper that Atkins subjects with ketosis had even greater increases in methylglyoxal 2.12 fold as well as acetol and acetone which increased 4.19 and 7.9 fold respectively so this is a study to show that methylglyoxal has been measured in patients doing an Atkins diet both a low carb version and a ketogenic version and chunks.title: Alternative Diets for Weight Loss
chunks.summary: The speaker addresses misconceptions about weight loss on a ketogenic diet, emphasizing that one can lose weight while consuming carbohydrates by improving food quality. They stress the importance of nutrition choices over the restrictive nature of ketosis.
chunks.transcript: you but I think it's important that we understand the whole perspective when we are making a decision about how to structure our diets for Optimal Health optimal aging optimal longevity refer to the longevity podcast I did last year if you're curious about how an animal-based diet which is what I do advocate for which is like a carnivore diet but includes organs and meat also fruit honey raw dairy other sources of carbohydrates so this is my Evolution as many of you who are familiar with my work will know so those are the two major problems I see with ketosis and there are many minor problems electrolyte imbalances hormones Section 1 binding globulin sleep disturbances all things that I talked about earlier but the major ones are cortisol connected with 11 beta hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1. and methylglyoxal

chunks.title: The Importance of Balanced Nutrition
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to advocating for a balanced approach to diet, particularly promoting an animal-based diet that includes organ meats and other sources of carbohydrates. The speaker references their own evolution in dietary philosophy and the benefits of this more inclusive approach.

chunks.title: Summarizing Ketogenic Diet Concerns
chunks.summary: The transcript concludes with a summary of the two primary concerns regarding the ketogenic diet: the rise in cortisol levels and the increase in methylglyoxal. The speaker highlights other minor issues and encourages listeners to consider their dietary choices for optimal health.
chunks.transcript: there are many minor problems electrolyte imbalances hormones Section 1 binding globulin sleep disturbances all things that I talked about earlier but the major ones are cortisol connected with 11 beta hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1. and methylglyoxal so again that is the framework of this
